title: Maximum Contiguous Subarray (Kadane)

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-07-algorithms-numerical-methods.md

setup.narrative: Given an array A[1..n] (positive AND negative values), find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray A[i..j].
rules: Naive O(n^2): for each i, j sweep all sums.
Prefix sums T(j) = A[1] + ... + A[j]; sub-array sum A[i..j] = T(j) - T(i-1).
For fixed j, max over i is T(j) - min_{k < j} T(k). Track both as we sweep -- O(n).
setup.question: Implement the O(n) algorithm and apply it to A = [1, 2, -5, 4, -3, 2, 6, -5, -1].

learningObjectives: Express subarray sums via prefix sums.
Track running min of prefix sums to compute the answer in O(n).
Map to financial use: max drawup / drawdown of a P&L curve.

stages.label: Reduce to prefix sums
stages.prompt: Let T(0) = 0, T(j) = A[1] + ... + A[j]. Then A[i..j] = T(j) - T(i-1). For fixed j, what minimizes T(i-1)?

options.text: T_min(j) := min_{0 <= k <= j-1} T(k).  Then max_{i <= j} A[i..j] = T(j) - T_min(j).

stages.explanation: We want T(i-1) as small as possible. Track the running min over prefix sums seen so far. Maintain T_min as we sweep j.

stages.label: Apply to example
stages.prompt: A = [1, 2, -5, 4, -3, 2, 6, -5, -1]. Compute prefix sums T = [0, 1, 3, -2, 2, -1, 1, 7, 2, 1]. The minimum of T (excluding the last) is T_min = -2 at index 3. The max T(j) - T_min for j > 3 is at j = 7 with T(7) = 7. Max subarray?
stages.explanation: Best j: T(7) = 7. Best i-1: T(3) = -2 (since this T_min was achieved at index 3). So i = 4 and the subarray is A[4..7] = [4, -3, 2, 6] summing to 9.

stages.label: Commit
stages.explanation: Max sum = 9 from subarray [4, -3, 2, 6]. Single O(n) sweep with two variables: T (running prefix sum) and T_min (running minimum so far).

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: What if all elements are negative? (The 'best' subarray is the single largest element; track this edge case.)
Map to financial drawdown: peak-to-trough P&L using the same algorithm on the negated returns.
Generalize to MAX SUBMATRIX in 2D: O(n^3) by reducing to many 1D max-subarray problems.
